description: 'nekuma' is animal flesh prepared, sold, or eaten as food. The noun leaves the animal source visible without deciding how the food was obtained or prepared. 'mashuri' (flesh) is the wider bodily substance, whether or not anyone treats it as food.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and release into middle-height front unrounded 'e'. Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release into stressed high-back rounded 'u'. Close the lips for voiced 'm', keep the sound nasal, and finish with central 'a'.
usage_notes: A source animal can modify 'nekuma' when the distinction matters. Preparation and preservation stay separate; so do sharing, refusal, and the circumstances of the animal's death.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The plain food noun keeps the animal source available to attention when a speaker describes a meal.

semantic_domains.nature: animal tissue understood through the species and body from which it came
semantic_domains.physical: animal flesh handled or eaten as food
